title: Peppery Roasted Chinese Twice Cooked Pork
description: A peppery and chewy Chinese dish featuring twice-cooked pork, perfect for a brunch mood, originating from the Fujian province. This recipe uses cornstarch and is gluten-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together cornstarch, black peppercorns, and Sichuan peppercorns.
Rub the cornstarch mixture all over the pork belly, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a kadhai over medium-high heat and sear the pork belly until browned on all sides.
Transfer the pork belly to a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
Remove the pork belly from the oven and let it cool.
Slice the pork belly into thin strips and set aside.
In the same kadhai, add the sliced ginger and minced garlic and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the soy sauce and Shaoxing wine to the kadhai and bring to a boil.
Add the sliced pork belly back to the kadhai and stir-fry until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chopped scallions and serve hot.
